Ad Text:
Are you ready to shape the future of data architecture and help build a modern, scalable, and insights-driven enterprise?

We’re seeking an experienced Data Architect to lead the design and delivery of next-generation data solutions — from data lakes and integration frameworks to advanced analytics platforms.

As the Data Architect, you’ll play a key role in defining and delivering the organisation’s enterprise data architecture. In addition, you’ll ensure data is structured, governed, and leveraged effectively to support operational and strategic decision-making.

You’ll provide architectural leadership across integration, data warehousing, analytics, and governance while fostering collaboration between business units, technical teams, and senior leadership.

Key Responsibilities of the Data Architect will include:

Develop and maintain enterprise-wide data architecture strategies, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with business goals.
Lead solution design for data platforms, integrations, data lakes, and BI/reporting systems using modern architectures (batch, streaming, APIs, microservices).
Create conceptual, logical, and physical data models across structured and unstructured data using best-practice frameworks (Inmon, Kimball, Data Vault).
Define and enforce data standards for quality, metadata, security, privacy, and compliance.
Architect data solutions across Azure, Snowflake, Informatica, and hybrid/multi-cloud environments.
Collaborate with stakeholders and technical teams to translate business needs into data-driven solutions.
Guide and mentor engineering teams on CI/CD, automation, and modern data engineering practices.
Optimise data systems for performance, scalability, and reliability.
Promote and embed a strong data culture across the organisation.
Skills & Experience required to measure success:

5 + years’ experience in data architecture, solution design, and enterprise data management.
Proven success architecting large-scale BI, analytics, and integration platforms.
Strong knowledge of cloud and hybrid ecosystems (Azure, AWS, Snowflake, Informatica, Databricks).
Expertise in data modelling (Inmon, Kimball, Data Vault 2.0).
Experience with data engineering tools, CI/CD, and DevOps practices.
Deep understanding of governance, metadata, and master data management.
Experience with modern integration approaches (APIs, event-driven, streaming).
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and leadership capability

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field (required).
Postgraduate qualification in Data Science or Information Systems (highly regarded).
Certifications in Microsoft, Azure, Snowflake, or Informatica (desirable).
Apply now if you’re passionate about leading enterprise-scale data architecture and building data ecosystems that enable real business impact!

Analytics Solution Engineer

Help children have the best start in life through high quality education & care.
Improve outcomes for children and families in early education and care.
Join an expert team working across governments at the national level.

About ACECQA

We want children in Australia to have the best start in life through quality education and care.

We provide national leadership on the implementation of the National Quality Framework (NQF) and collaborate with the Australian and state and territory governments to:

implement changes that benefit children birth to 13 years-of-age and their families
monitor and promote the consistent application of the Education and Care Services National Law across all states and territories
support the early childhood education and care sector to improve quality outcomes for children

We strive for innovation and continuous improvement and are committed to keeping the sector and the community informed with the latest developments in early childhood education and care.

ACECQA is committed to being a child-safe organisation, implementing the National Principles for Child Safe Organisations and actively promoting the safety and wellbeing of children.

The Role

The Analytics Solution Engineer is required to apply subject matter expertise and professional judgement to deliver practical, value-driven analytics solutions that support insight generation, reporting, and informed decision-making for stakeholders of the National Quality Agenda IT System (NQA ITS).

The role is responsible for translating complex business and regulatory requirements into effective analytical products, leveraging data integration, data modelling, enterprise reporting, and advanced analytics using Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and modern data warehousing approaches. As a hands-on practitioner this role is also required to develop and demonstrate early-stage AI/ML proof-of-concepts and applied solutions, with a clear focus on delivering tangible business value.

This role is full-time and fixed term to 30 June 2027 at ACECQA Band 6 ($126,436- $140,976).

About You

To be successful in this role you will need:

High level of proficiency across the Power BI and Microsoft Fabric stack, covering both data ingestion and warehousing
Demonstrated experience applying data science or machine-learning techniques in an analytics or business intelligence context
Ability to explain why particular models and parameters have or should be used.
Proficiency in Python, SQL, and data visualisation tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau).
Strong practical understanding of statistical analysis and predictive modelling techniques.
Experience working with cloud platforms (Azure preferred).
Excellent problem-solving, communication, and stakeholder engagement skills.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.
Tertiary qualifications in a relevant discipline and/or an equivalent level of industry experience.

While not essential, the following attributes will greatly assist you in the role:

Experience in government, education, or regulated sectors.
Familiarity with data governance frameworks and ethical AI principles.
Certifications in data science or cloud technologies (e.g. Microsoft Certified: Azure Data Scientist Associate).
Microsoft Certifications across Fabric such as DP600 and DP700

ACECQA is committed to removing barriers and opening up opportunities. As an employer we seek to ensure people with differing needs and abilities have the same opportunities to successfully gain skills, knowledge and experience through work, social activities, education and training irrespective of their age, disability, colour, race, gender, religion, sexuality, family responsibilities, or location. In particular, we are committed to improving opportunities and outcomes for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.

You must have the right to work and live in Australia to apply for this role.

The successful candidate will be required to consent to a national police check.

How to apply

The closing date for applications is 31 May 2026 at 11.59pm.

Important Note: Please ensure you follow the recruitment process outlined below, otherwise you may not be considered for the role.

To apply, please register with the ACECQA Recruitment Candidate Portal, navigate to Analytics Solution Engineer vacancy and click Apply for Job. The application process will require you to:

Attach a CV of no more than 5 pages which demonstrates your ability to meet the criteria contained in the position description.
Provide the details of at least two referees who have directly supervised you within the last five years of employment.
Attach a written response to the targeted questions below of no more than 2 pages

Targeted Questions

Describe an end-to-end analytics solution you've built using Microsoft Fabric or Azure (ADF/Synapse).Include how you handled data ingestion, transformation, storage (Lakehouse/Warehouse), and reporting.
Describe a practical use of AI or machine learning that you've implemented or prototyped. What was the use case, what tools did you use, and how could a similar approach be applied in a small-to-medium data environment?

All complete applications will be acknowledged.

More information

If you have any questions about the role or recruitment process, please contact the Recruitment team at recruitment@ acecqa.gov.au.

Notice to agencies

ACECQA will not accept applications from any source other than directly from a candidate for this vacancy. Recruitment agencies must have received instructions from ACECQA in relation to a specific vacancy in order to submit applications on behalf of candidates.

ACECQA is collecting the information requested in the advertisement for the purpose of selection, recruitment and engagement of staff. ACECQA is authorised to do so by the Education and Care Services National Law. Please refer to our Privacy Policy which can be accessed at http://www.acecqa.gov.au/privacy-policy.

Your role

As one of our Senior Robotics Engineers, you will play a key role in the development and rollout of our next-generation tethered drone platform, working across design, prototyping, integration, testing, and real-world deployment. The role is intentionally broad and suited to engineers who enjoy solving multidisciplinary problems and taking ownership of complex systems.

Requirements

Bachelor of Engineering in Electrical, Mechanical, Mechatronics, Aerospace, Robotics, or similar discipline

Strong hands-on experience with UAVs, robotics, autonomous systems, USVs, or related electromechanical platforms

Experience with PX4 and/or Ardupilot highly regarded

Experience integrating and debugging sensors, motors, embedded hardware, and control systems

Understanding of telemetry, communications, and real-world system integration

Experience with field testing, validation, rapid prototyping and productionisation

Strong troubleshooting and practical problem-solving ability

Comfortable working across mechanical, electrical, and software domains

Decomposing complex mission requirements into structured engineering tasks

Australian work authorisation is required for this position

We'd Especially Like to Hear From People With Experience In

UAV/drone systems

Robotics or autonomous systems

USVs or marine robotics

Aerospace or defense systems

Embedded systems

High-reliability electromechanical systems

Rapid prototyping and R&D environments

Compliance, verification, and system testing

Experience with OnShape and/or Solidworks

Networked UAV communication systems using MAVLink over UDP/TCP

Embedded Linux

Candidates will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis considering the combination of skills, experience, and potential fit for the role rather than strict adherence to all listed qualifications.

Please include short answers to the below:

Tell us about the most relevant physical hardware system you've helped build, test, troubleshoot or improve. What was your role?

Have you worked on a product moving from prototype, beta or R&D stage into repeatable production? What did you personally help improve?

What hands-on engineering skills do you bring? For example: mechanical assembly, CAD, wiring, soldering, test rigs, fabrication, fault finding, production documentation, servicing or field support.

Tell us about a time you found and fixed a difficult engineering problem. What was the issue, and how did you solve it?

What kind of engineering work are you strongest at, and where do you still want to grow?

Job Title: Data Engineer - Azure, Fabric & Data Pipelines
Category:
Engineering - Software (Information & Communication Technology)
Other (Education & Training)
Ad Text:
Join us at a pivotal moment of growth

Box Hill Institute is entering a significant period of growth and transformation, with major investment underway across our IT and AI implementation space. We're building capability, embedding smarter ways of working, and leveraging emerging technologies to support our people, students and future workforce.

APPLY NOW and be part of what's next.

Shape the future of data and AI at Box Hill Institute

Join a purpose-driven organisation where your work directly supports learners, educators and communities. As a Data Engineer, you'll design, build and operate secure, scalable data pipelines that power analytics, reporting and AI across Box Hill Institute. Working with Microsoft Fabric and Azure, you'll create trusted data assets that enable confident, data-driven decisions.

If you thrive on solving complex technical challenges, love working collaboratively and want to make your mark in a modern, evolving data environment - this is the opportunity for you.

What You'll Do

Design, build and operate data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric and Azure using approved ETL/ELT patterns.
Deliver reliable pipelines, feature sets, embeddings and curated datasets to support analytics and AI workloads.
Manage Fabric operations including workloads, schedules, connectors, configurations, secrets and version control.
Ensure data quality, governance and security through validation, secure coding, access controls and metadata management.
Maintain documentation including runbooks, schemas, data dictionaries, lineage and technical procedures.
Collaborate with BI/Analytics, Applications, Cyber Security, Infrastructure and AI teams on integrated solutions and Agile initiatives.
Provide operational support by triaging tickets, resolving incidents, clarifying requirements, supporting UAT, training and BAU transition.

What You'll Bring

Proven experience building and running scalable data pipelines for analytics and AI.
Strong ETL/ELT skills across varied data formats.
Hands-on capability with Microsoft Azure, Microsoft Fabric and modern integration tools (APIs, event streams, iPaaS).
Solid engineering foundations - Git, CI/CD, testing, logging and monitoring.
A clear understanding of data quality, governance and security practices.
Confidence in production support, incident resolution and performance optimisation.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to simplify technical concepts for non-technical audiences.

Qualifications/Experience

A relevant qualification in IT, Computer Science, Information Systems or Data Engineering/Analytics (Azure/Fabric certifications a bonus).
Demonstrated experience designing and operating scalable data pipelines and ETL/ELT
Hands-on skills with Azure data services, Microsoft Fabric and modern integration tools (APIs, event streams, iPaaS).

Why work with us?

At Box Hill Institute, we offer a supportive and rewarding work environment with benefits that are designed to help you thrive both professionally and personally:

Flexible work arrangements
On-site parking
Generous leave options such as Emergency response leave and up to 16 weeks paid Parental Leave
Health & wellbeing supports and resources, including FREE flu-vaccinations, FREE skin checks, FREE health checks and corporate health insurance discounts
Financial perks like novated leasing and salary packaging
Unique campus experiences to support student learning
Exclusive staff discounts on CAE/Box Hill Institute short courses for staff and their immediate family

APPLY NOW

All applications must be submitted online and include a cover letter and current resume.

For specific enquiries relating to this position, please contact it-•••••••@boxhill.edu.au

Applications close: 5:00pm, Friday 12th June 2026

Please note that applications will be assessed as they are received, and an appointment may be made prior to the closing date.

We are a 2025 Circle Back Initiative Employer - we commit to respond to every applicant.

Box Hill Institute is a welcoming, safe and respectful work environment. We are work-life friendly and an equal opportunity employer. We encourage people from all backgrounds, abilities and identities to apply for our vacancies.

Box Hill Institute is a Child Safe Organisation and has a zero tolerance towards the harm or abuse of children and young people. We believe that child safety is everyone's responsibility. All employees, volunteers, contractors and service providers are required to promote a culture of child safety, comply with the Child Safe Standards and other relevant legislation and reporting obligations of suspected child abuse as per the Institute's Child Safety Policy and Procedures. All employees are required to hold and maintain a current, valid Working with Children Check for the duration of their employment at their own expense.

Candidates for employment at Box Hill Institute will be subject to pre-employment probity checks, and must satisfy Box Hill Institute's pre-conditions for employment.

Box Hill Institute reserves the right to withdraw an advertised position at any stage.

We are seeking a hands-on Data Platform Lead to own and evolve a modern cloud-native data environment. You will lead the design and delivery of scalable data engineering solutions, enabling real-time data processing, advanced analytics, AI/ML capability, and self-service reporting across the organisation.

This role will focus on driving innovation through modern data platforms, Generative AI, and automation while establishing robust data architecture, pipelines, governance, and engineering best practices.

You and your team will be responsible for bringing together structured and unstructured data from multiple sources and transforming it into meaningful insights for advanced analytics and AI initiatives.

You will also play a key role in establishing and growing cutting-edge AI capabilities as the organisation continues its journey towards embedding AI into its services and operations.

What You'll Bring Benfits
Strong experience across data engineering, data architecture, and cloud-native platforms (Azure preferred)
Exposure to Fabric, Databricks, and Snowflake highly desirable
Proven experience leading enterprise data platform initiatives
Expertise in data modelling, ETL/ELT pipelines, data warehousing, and real-time processing
Exposure to AI/ML platforms, Generative AI, and MLOps practices
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Passion for innovation, continuous improvement, and modern data capability uplift

This is an exciting opportunity for someone who considers themselves an experienced, hands-on leader within the data space and is looking for their next challenge.

Please apply by uploading your CV, and I will be in touch to discuss the role further.

Location: Wollongong, Wollongong, Illawarra & South Coast NSW (Hybrid)
Ad Text:
ABOUT GM³

GM³ is an emerging Australian metallurgical coal mining entity owned by global investment entity Golden Energy and Resources (GEAR) and M Resources, a market leader in metallurgical coal industry services.

Illawarra Metallurgical Coal (IMC) produces premium quality, hard coking coal for steelmaking, which is transported within Australia and around the world. We operate two underground metallurgical coal mines, two preparation plants and a logistics chain.  

WHAT GM³ OFFERS

Options for additional superannuation contributions

Annual short-term incentive bonus that recognises both your individual performance and the overall success of the business

Hybrid working environment

ABOUT THE ROLE 

This position will be based at our Regional Operations Centre, Port Kembla Coal Terminal in the coastal city of Wollongong, NSW, approximately 75 kilometres southwest of Sydney.

Reporting to the Lead Data, you will help drive value and innovation in data, analytics and AI.

The Senior Analytics Engineer plays a critical role in designing, building, and maintaining robust data models and semantic layers that enable accurate, efficient, and scalable analytics across the organisation. This role bridges the gap between data engineering and analytics, ensuring that business stakeholders have access to trusted, well-structured data for reporting and decision-making.

You will help with development and delivery of data products, maintenance of a central leading edge Data Platform, improving the quality of data in source applications, increasing data literacy throughout the organisation and building a foundation of data that can be leveraged for advanced analytics, machine learning and artificial intelligence (AI)

Key responsibilities include, but aren’t limited to:

Data Modelling:

Design / implement conceptual, logical, and physical data models using SQL-based technologies

Develop and maintain Power BI semantic models

Design and implement AI ready models to support Agentic and Generative AI

Collaboration & Governance:

Define data models that represent business processes.

Review and approve vendor code contributions (pull requests).

Support vendors in delivering high-quality models and code aligned with best practices.

Development & Optimisation:

Build and maintain a metrics / KPI layer in SQL; assist in selecting and implementing a future dedicated metrics layer (dbt metrics etc.)

Optimise query performance for large-scale and streaming data models.

Develop and manage CI/CD pipelines using GitHub and/or Azure DevOps

Support & Enablement:

Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to data models and semantic layers.

Provide coaching and guidance to analysts and end users on model usage.

Create and maintain comprehensive documentation for data models and processes.

Collaborate with OT (Operational technology) engineers and functional analysts to ensure the roadmap and strategy of Data & Analytics is aligned to their needs and realistic 

Additional Responsibilities:

Power BI dashboard development as needed.

Assist in big data analytics and streaming solutions

An understanding of time series historians and/or PostGres SQL is nice to have

ABOUT YOU

You are an Analytics Engineer who can interpret complex requirements and translate them into efficient models. You have great attention to detail to ensure accuracy and consistency in data models. 

You work effectively with business stakeholders, vendors and internal teams. You are skilled at troubleshooting data issues and can explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical audiences.

You are comfortable working with evolving technologies and take accountability and ownership over deliverables and quality standards. 

You will have experience and demonstrate capability in the following:

Expert proficiency in SQL for data modelling, stored procedures and query optimization.

Hands-on experience building complex PowerBI models & dashboards (or equivalent)

Knowledge of CI/CD pipelines using Azure DevOps or GitHub.

Technical skills and knowledge of data modelling and data integration

Proven experience in building and maintaining enterprise-scale data models.

Experience translating business requirements into technical solutions.

Prior involvement in vendor management and code review processes.

Exposure to big data environments and streaming analytics preferred.

Nice to have:

Experience with dbt for data transformation and modelling.

Familiarity with Azure Data Factory, Synapse Analytics, Microsoft Fabric or similar

Understanding of big data and streaming technologies (Event Hubs, ADX)

Qualifications, skills, and competencies:

Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Data Analytics, Information Systems, Engineering or a related field (or equivalent experience).

Strong understanding of data modelling principles and analytics engineering best practices.

3+ years in data engineering, analytics engineering, or BI development roles.

You must have a drivers license and vehicle to be able to drive to site

Ready to make a real impact? Apply now and help shape the future of our Cloud & Infrastructure capability.

GM³ is committed to building and maintaining an inclusive and diverse workforce that reflects the communities in which we operate.  We provide a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply. 

This job advert will close on 29 May

Applications will be assessed as they are received, and this advertisement may close early if a suitable candidate pool is identified.

The System Engineer role will form part of multi-disciplined project teams to design and deliver complex mission-critical voice and data systems. The systems may be used directly by our customers or by Motorola Solutions to deliver Managed Services to our customers. The System Engineer will work in the post-sales phases of projects and may support multiple projects in parallel, depending on size and complexity. There may also be a requirement to provide support to pre-sales activities.

The System Engineer role responsibilities will include the types of activities listed below;

Validating the client’s needs & requirements against pre-sales design

Perform detailed design to refine the required system & service solutions

Design reviews in accordance with the Technical Staff process.

Bill of Materials and equipment ordering

Generation of system-specific technical documentation to project quality standards and processes

Working with product groups & 3rd Parties to ensure equipment and sub-systems are available to meet project requirements and timescales.

Requirements Management.

Providing technical support for the deployment of the project

Timely detection and resolution of deviations from the design.

Participating in project reviews and identifying risks, issues and lessons learned

Adhering to applicable processes and maintaining accurate records.

Ensuring systems meet quality and security standards.

Management and closure of open technical issues to the customer’s satisfaction.

Proactively reporting back into the organisation and escalating where appropriate.

Developing and/or executing test strategies and plans to ensure the solution(s) work as designed and requirements are fulfilled.

Manage and/or perform implementation of APCO P25 communication system solutions and services which satisfy the requirements of the customer, end user and operator

Provides technical support for Solution Staging, as required

Lead or participate in Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) while coordinating the technical aspects of the acceptance testing.

Responsible for the installation, integration, testing, and commissioning of MSI and 3rd party solutions (with support from the supplier/vendor)

Responsible for identifying, investigating and troubleshooting technical issues during project implementation

Responsible for on-site Acceptance Tests, while coordinating the technical aspects of the acceptance testing.

Update of technical documentation and technical change management

Provide technical support during all project phases through to handover to the Support organisation

General Education

Essential:

University degree or equivalent education in one of the following faculties: Electronic and Telecommunication Engineering, Telecommunication Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Network Engineering, Computer Systems Engineering

Professional Experience

Essential:

University degree or equivalent education in one of the following faculties: Electronic and Telecommunication Engineering, Telecommunication Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Network Engineering, Computer Systems Engineering

At least 5 years of professional experience in the area, e.g. Computer System and Network Engineer, Telecommunications Engineer, Field Network Engineer, Field Service Engineer, Customer Support Engineer or equivalent

Desirable:

Experience in one or more of the following would be an added advantage: 

Radio Communication Systems, Command and Control Systems, Technical Analysis and Problem

Troubleshooting;

System integration and testing experience in Motorola-specific technology, i.e. ASTRO P25, DIMETRA TETRA, MOTOTRBO DMR and NICE Voice Logging

More Information:

Hours of Workday per week: 37.5 Hours Per Week

Proposed Salary range: 110,000.00 - 125,000.00

Location: Ascot, Western Australia

Ad Expiry: 15 June 2026

Category:
Workplace Training & Assessment (Education & Training)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Technical Trainer

APS6

Melbourne

Position overview

The Observing Operations Training group supports the Bureau's observations programs through the development and delivery of specialised training and assessment for observing operations staff.

The occupant of this position will deliver training on systems and procedures, relating to the rollout of a new data gathering system (automatic weather station- AWS) which is occurring in the next twelve months. The duties will include development of new course material as well as the updating of current training content. The material will be developed and delivered in a variety of ways; through our learning management system (Moodle) or the creation of web tools, videos and documentation.

The position will be based in Melbourne; however the occupant may occasionally be required to travel interstate to participate in regionally based training activities. Additionally, from time to time, the occupant will undertake course management activities associated with the range of short technical courses conducted throughout the year.

An industry accredited adult education qualification, equivalent to at least Certificate 4 level is currently a requirement of undertaking training and assessment activities. Holding a Certificate IV in Workplace Training and Assessment is an advantage

The occupant will be expected to have a broad knowledge of the Bureau's operations and have substantial experience in the maintenance and repair of electronic and electro-mechanical equipment.  A good understanding of theoretical electronics, IT and their practical applications is necessary (including current Bureau operational systems and software).  The occupant will be expected to contribute to training of staff in the use of current Bureau software-based management systems such as SAP, EAMS and EDMS, and make effective use of these systems in his or her other training.

A measured but flexible approach to training and education is preferred, as the duties include much interaction with varied groups of adult learners.  It is expected that the successful applicant will have demonstrated an aptitude or interest in furthering their academic studies and personal development opportunities.

Role responsibilities

The responsibilities of the role include but are not limited to:

Complying with all Bureau work, health and safety policies and procedures, and taking reasonable care for your own health and safety and that of employees, contractors and visitors who may be affected by your conduct.
Organise, coordinate, and supervise the training and assessment of appropriate Bureau staff in the repair and maintenance of Bureau observing sites and equipment to the standard outlined in Bureau procedures and adhering to relevant safety policies and procedures.
Liaise with external providers to deliver appropriate training required to Bureau staff
Develop and deliver online and face to face learning materials and remote training sessions using the Bureau’s Learning Management System (LMS) ensuring alignment to adult learning methodologies.
Consult with other areas of the Bureau to ensure training is up to date and aligns with Bureau practices.
Provide training and assessment advice to Bureau staff in head office and regional hubs

Application Closing Time and Date: 11:30pm, Friday 29 May 2026

For further information please review the position description on https://bomcareers.nga.net.au/?jati=38E88D3A-9433-C9DD-72D6-ECEFAAB9433F

For over 40 years, our shared commitment to curing childhood cancer has united us. Together, we continue to accelerate the answers to make that possible.

To accomplish our vision, we’re seeking someone who lives our values: owning and delivering meaningful work, collaborating to drive impact, challenging the status quo to create a better future, and balancing logic with empathy. Let’s achieve more, together.

The Computational Biology Group leads a diverse portfolio of projects in precision medicine, genomics, and bioinformatics, spanning novel methods development, data systems, and the generation of biological and clinical insights from large-scale multi-omic datasets. We are looking for a Bioinformatics Research Engineer to:

Develop and implement computational systems for precision medicine, enabling the extraction, integration, and delivery of clinically relevant knowledge from diverse data sources.
Design knowledge-driven architectures, including knowledge graphs and inference mechanisms, to model domain knowledge and support scalable, automated curation workflows.
Apply and evaluate AI-driven approaches, including LLM-based workflows, to enhance knowledge extraction, synthesis, and interaction across clinical and research datasets.
Collaborate across engineering, curation, and research teams to integrate solutions into production workflows, while contributing to research outputs and mentoring students.

To succeed in this role you will have…

Master’s degree in bioinformatics, software engineering, computer science, computational biology or a related discipline
3+ years' experience in precision medicine or related roles with demonstrated expertise in bioinformatics, computational biology and/or software development
A working understanding of graph-based data systems or knowledge graphs, with hands-on experience designing and maintaining knowledge graphs in production or research settings a plus
Experience applying AI/ML techniques, including NLP or large language models, in a research or data-driven context
Experience working collaboratively in multidisciplinary teams spanning research, engineering, and clinical domains

We will support you to be your best with benefits that include but are not limited to:

Working in a world-class, purpose-built facility, the only one in Australia dedicated to curing childhood cancer
Focus on employee wellbeing, including up to 5 gifted wellbeing days off (paid) per year
Access to Fitness Passport, with access to an extensive choice of fitness facilities across Sydney at discounted rates
Corporate discounts and discounts on education through UNSW

Join us to shape a future every child deserves and to find meaning in your own. Because together, we can cure every child of cancer. It’s not if, it’s when.

Additional information about Children's Cancer Institute can be found on our website at: https://www.ccia.org.au/jobs-study . We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Children’s Cancer Institute and our culture.  We embrace diversity and encourage you to advise of any reasonable adjustments you may need during the recruitment process.
